title: Pedantic nitpickery regarding proper man page markdown formatting
body:
Been going through the podman man pages, and a couple questions about proper markdown formatting (not being an expert at markdown).

First, commonly, one sees a pair of options rendered in bold thusly:

`**--help, -h**`

However, would it not make more sense to not bold the intervening comma and render that as:

`**--help**, **-h**`

I've definitely seen that second variant and it makes more logical sense to me.

Also, in podman-container-exists.1.md, I read:

```
# SYNOPSIS
**podman container exists** [*-h*|*--help*] *container*
```
For the same markdown results, would you not use double asterisks instead of single in the last part of that line? Again, I am not a markdown expert so I might just not understand the effect you're going after.

comments.body:
The only difference between **--help, -h**  and **--help**, **-h** is the "," will be boded in the first and not in the second.  I think using the first throughout is preferable.

**podman container exists** [*-h*|*--help*] *container*
Should be
**podman container exists** [-h|--help] container

The extra "*" make no sense.

OK, so based on my example, your preferred way of writing it would be:
```
# SYNOPSIS
**podman container exists** [*-h*|*--help*] *container*
```
that is, two asterisks around the command, single asterisks around the subsequent things?
Oh, and just to be pedantic, my normal preferred way to render that comma would have it *not* bolded, but that requires a bunch more asterisks and it's not worth it.

Actually it should be 
**podman container exists** [*options*] *container*
None of the --help ones should exist.
Sorry, I've been distracted, will get to this at some point.
Ashley could you look into this also.

It seems like the man pages for podman are split, some using [options] and others listing it all out. I took a look at `man man` and it gave an outline of how manual pages should be written. It looks like all the options were written on top, not just [options]. However, as seen in podman-image-trust, this can look pretty messy.

Here are some of the usages: 

       bold text          type exactly as shown.
       italic text        replace with appropriate argument.
       [-abc]             any or all arguments within [ ] are optional.
       -a|-b              options delimited by | cannot be used together.
       argument ...       argument is repeatable.
       [expression] ...   entire expression within [ ] is repeatable
@ashley-cui Yes we are just looking for you to run through them and make them consistent.
So should I just change them all to the [options] format?
I think so, Could you show me an example of the change?

So instead of 
```
# SYNOPSIS
**podman image trust** set|show
[**-h**|**--help**]
[**-j**|**--json**]
[**--raw**]
[**-f**|**--pubkeysfile** KEY1 [**-f**|**--pubkeysfile** KEY2,...]]
[**-t**|**--type** signedBy|accept|reject]
REGISTRY[/REPOSITORY]

# OPTIONS
**-h** **--help**
  Print usage statement.
etc
```
I have

```
# SYNOPSIS
podman image trust set|show [options]

# OPTIONS
**-h,--help**
Print usage statement

**-j,--json **
Output trust as JSON for machine parsing

**--raw**
Output trust policy file as raw JSON
```

Sorry for the lack of attention, I've been pre-occupied with a current contract. If @ashley-cui has a handle on this, I'm more than happy to hand it off, and go find something else nitpicky to whine about.

@rpjday No problem.  @ashley-cui is a summer intern and we are bringing her up to speed on the whole libpod/podman process.  Grabbing issues for her to work on.
Thanks for the issue.

The only nitpick I would have with the above is, instead of
`**-h,--help**`
I would avoid bolding the comma, as in:
`**-h**,**--help**`
as any punctuation like that is not formally part of the option string. The same would apply to, say, a vertical bar between options, and so on.
